provider_scope: pre-production candidate boundary for controlled provider execution; no live provider write in v9.2
credential_boundary: Credential source, scope, storage, redaction, expiry, and rotation must be reviewed before any provider execution.
budget_boundary: Maximum call count, maximum spend, timeout, and quota owner must be set before any provider execution.
revocation_boundary: Human approval can be revoked before execution; stale or revoked approval blocks execution.
rollback_boundary: Rollback proof must be ready and linked before execution; rollback readiness is not rollback execution.
monitoring_boundary: Operator must be able to observe start, target, result, failure, and stop signals.
incident_boundary: Incident owner, escalation route, stop condition, and recovery evidence must be defined.
human_go_no_go_boundary: A human operator must explicitly decide go/no-go against the full boundary packet.
product_value_comprehension_boundary: Release value must be understandable; unclear value blocks production execution claims.
stop_conditions: Stop if production execution authorization is claimed by v9.2.
Stop if any credential, budget, revocation, rollback, monitoring, incident, human go/no-go, or product-value boundary is missing.
not_proven: This boundary proves pre-production eligibility structure only, not production execution safety, provider correctness, credential safety, billing safety, market value, or actual rollback execution.
